heading: Monday Night
text: Becoming cloudy. A rising chance of rain showers after midnight, with some pockets of freezing rain in the St. Lawrence Valley. Lows in the 30s to low 40s from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, in the 40s south. Winds west, becoming north, north of Rt. 4, diminishing to light and variable.

heading: Tuesday
text: Rain begins in the early morning from west to east. Pockets of freezing rain or sleet near and north of the Quebec border, then periods of rain. A lull during the afternoon then another wave arrives in the late evening. Highs from the 40s to low 50s from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, upper 50s and 60s south. Light winds, mainly east from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, to south for areas south of Rt. 4, near 10 mph.

heading: Tuesday Night
text: Periods of rain and showers, some freezing rain near and north of the Quebec border. Heavy at times over Central and Southern VT. Quite mild south. Lows in the 30s to low 40s north, 40s south. Winds east in northern areas, south to southwest in southern areas 10 to 15 mph.
